question: Looking at the US unemployment rate map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: Florida and West Virginia have similar unemployment rates.
options.B: The unemployment rate differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.
options.C: All highlighted states have very different unemployment rate levels.
options.D: The unemployment rate gap between Florida and West Virginia might exceed that between Texas and Illinois.

explanation: Florida (11.2%) and West Virginia (14.3%) have a 3.1% unemployment rate difference, while Texas (6.5%) and Illinois (7.3%) have only a 0.8% difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Florida and West Virginia into the same color bin (9%+ range), making their large difference appear similar, while Texas and Illinois appear more different due to different color bins.
